ops: by='target' → Suite.marks_for(target): every mark on a claim/span, oldest-first
by='type' → Suite.marks_by_type(mark_type): every mark of a kind across targets (REGISTRY-GATED, fail-loud on unknown type)
by='findings' → store.findings_for(address): the legacy coherence findings at a corpus address (DISTINCT store)
how_verified: Verified in ISOLATION (not via the whole server — sibling lanes mid-write). Built a throwaway FastMCP('verify') + a real Suite(FsStore(STORE_DIR), NodeRegistry().discover(nodes)), called marks.register(mcp, suite). Confirmed: (1) tool registers — asyncio.run(mcp.list_tools()) == ['marks'], register() returns fn named 'marks'. (2) Round-trip on a LIVE registered mark_type: suite.mark('run://verify-marks-lane/claim-1', 'ai_fingerprint', value=, confidence=, source_pass=, evidence=) then read back via by='target' (total=1, concise = mark_type/value/confidence/source_pass/evidence/ts) AND by='type' (total=1, concise = target/value/confidence/source_pass/evidence/ts) — proves the two retrieval keys + the per-branch concise shaping. (3) detail='detailed' returns the full 7-field record. (4) by='findings' on an arbitrary address returns an honest empty list (total=0). (5) Error paths return teaching dicts: unknown by, missing target/mark_type/address, bad detail. (6) GATE: marks(by='type', mark_type='not_a_real_type_xyz') RAISES ValueError listing the registered types (because we wrap Suite.marks_by_type, NOT store.marks_by_type — the gate bites). (7) limit caps rows. No GPU needed — reads call no model. Live mark_types observed from suite.mark_type_registry: ['ai_fingerprint','built_twice','contradiction','gold_likelihood','overlap'] — enums derive from the live registry, nothing hardcoded.
notes: CQRS held: the WRITE tool `mark(target, mark_type, value, confidence, source_pass, evidence)` (server.py:1024) STAYS as-is — reported in flat_tools_to_keep, NOT removed. The flat-tool removal in mcp_face/server.py is the SERIAL pass after this sweep; do NOT remove `mark`. STORE ASYMMETRY (load-bearing, carried into the docstring so the cold external agent picks the right `by`): by='target'/by='type' read the MARKS store (marks.jsonl, keyed by claim/span target + REGISTERED mark_type) via Suite methods; by='findings' reads the DISTINCT legacy COHERENCE findings store (findings.jsonl, keyed by corpus address) via store.findings_for — there is NO Suite.findings_for, so we mirror the old flat tool's SUITE.store.findings_for reach exactly. SEAM (flagged, NOT built — out of this lane's single file; the create_projection in-lane precedent at server.py:910): the symmetric long-term home would be a Suite.findings_for wrapper mirroring Suite.marks_for, so by='findings' would also go through the Suite rather than reaching into .store. Recommend the SUITE lane add it later; new_suite_methods=[] for now (adding it would edit runtime/suite.py, out of lane). by='type' deliberately wraps Suite.marks_by_type (the gated path), NEVER store.marks_by_type, so registry-is-truth is enforced. The `by` enum ('target','type','findings') is the tool's own verb vocabulary (hardcoded tuple like corpus.py's OPS) — registry-is-truth bites on mark_type, which the Suite already gates against the live registry. Mirrors mcp_face/tools/corpus.py's pattern (op-selector, concise default, teaching errors) but with PER-BRANCH concise projection because marks {target,mark_type,value,confidence,source_pass,evidence,ts} and findings {kind,address,state,source,evidence,ts} have different schemas — corpus.py's single _shape would not fit both. FILE-DISJOINT respected: wrote ONLY mcp_face/tools/marks.py; did not touch server.py, sibling lane modules, or roles/AGENTS.md. Not committed (per instruction). detail=concise default + limit=100 keep responses token-efficient; no raw hashes/IDs in concise output.
